sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old mother of twins, injured her right knee while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She caught her ski on the lift and fell, causing her knee to pop and resulting in persistent pain.
She was unable to bear weight on the knee and was taken down the mountain by the ski patrol.
The patient reports pain on the outside of the knee and a feeling that it will buckle, with decreased flexion and extension.
She has been unable to straighten the knee fully.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and reports that she can feel her toes.
Past medical history: The patient reports being generally healthy, with no history of high blood pressure, diabetes, or other chronic conditions.
Review of systems: The patient reports being a relatively active person, chasing her two 4-year-old twins and enjoying activities such as skiing.
She has a supportive partner who is home with her, but works a job that requires him to be away from home for extended periods.
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee, with pain to palpation of the right lateral knee.
There is decreased flexion and extension of the right knee, and a positive Lachman sign.
The patient's heart sounds are regular, and her lungs are clear to auscultation.
The patient has palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses in both feet, with no leg edema in the ankle.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee, based on her symptoms and physical exam.
Problem list: ACL rupture; right knee pain; decreased flexion and extension of the right knee.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis and assess for any other potential injuries.
I recommended continued use of ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain, and a knee brace to provide support.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the MRI and determine the best course of treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again in a few days to discuss the results of the MRI and plan for further treatment.
